analysis.consistency: Consistent with standard D&D 5e feature structure for Bards gaining external spells.
analysis.quality: High quality; clearly defines the mechanics of learning spells from other classes.

mechanics.name: Magical Secrets
mechanics.spell_acquisition_rule: Choose two spells from any class list (including Bard). Spells must be of a level the Bard can cast (up to half their total level, or a cantrip). The chosen spells count as Bard spells for the purpose of spell slots and preparation/known lists.
mechanics.initial_acquisition_details: The initial acquisition is tied to reaching 10th level, not 14th level as implied by the raw data structure which only lists the 14th level trigger in the description text without the primary 10th level trigger fully detailed in the main structure fields. The description clearly states: 'By 10th level, you have plundered magical knowledge...' and then 'You learn two additional spells from any class at 14th level and again at 18th level.' This suggests the feature grants spells at 10th, 14th, and 18th levels.
mechanics.clarification_needed: The RAW DATA structure focuses on the 14th level instance, but the description details acquisition at 10th, 14th, and 18th. Assuming the feature is generally understood as granting spells at 10th, 14th, and 18th level.
